title: EDesignation
description: An (E)-designation (or its Restrictive Declaration equivalent) carried by a property as a consequence of a zoning action or City Environmental Quality Review (CEQR). It attaches environmental requirements — hazardous materials, noise, and/or air — that OER must sign off on before the Department of Buildings will issue a building permit and before a certificate of occupancy is granted. NOTE ON OWNERSHIP: the authoritative, machine-readable (E)-designation inventory is published on NYC Open Data under the DEPARTMENT OF CITY PLANNING (DCP) label ('E-Designations' hxm3-23vy / jsrs-ggnx, 'E Designations: shapefile' mzjp-98aw), not OER. OER is the agency that administers RESOLVING the (E) requirement. This schema reconciles the DCP-published designation record with OER's remedial-resolution view of it.

x-source.opendata_owner: Department of City Planning (DCP) — OER administers resolution, DCP publishes the inventory.

eNumber.description: The (E)-designation number assigned via the zoning action (e.g. 'E-123'). DCP's designation key.

eType.description: The category or categories of environmental requirement the designation attaches. OER manages three: Hazardous Materials, Noise, Air. A property may carry more than one.

instrument.description: Whether the requirement is carried as a zoning-map (E)-designation or as a recorded Restrictive Declaration against title.

ceqrNumber.description: Associated City Environmental Quality Review (CEQR) number, where the requirement derives from an environmental review.

zoningAction.description: The rezoning / ULURP action that created the designation.

resolved.description: Whether OER has issued the sign-off (Notice of No Objection / Notice of Satisfaction) that clears the requirement for building-permit and certificate-of-occupancy purposes.
